name: St. James's Hospital
description: One of Ireland's largest acute teaching hospitals, serving the greater Dublin area.
accessibilityNotes: Fully step-free campus with dedicated drop-off zones beside main entrance; all wards served by wide-door lifts rated for bariatric wheelchairs; RADAR-key accessible toilets on every floor.

name: Beaumont Hospital
description: Major north Dublin hospital and regional centre for neurosurgery and renal services.
accessibilityNotes: Large designated wheelchair parking bays within 30m of main entrance; new outpatient wing (2019) is fully step-free; older wards in Block C have narrower corridors but remain navigable.

name: Tallaght University Hospital
description: Purpose-built acute hospital serving south-west Dublin and the Midlands.
accessibilityNotes: Entire hospital built to universal design standards from opening in 1998; ambulance and taxi set-down is level with entrance lobby; all lifts have audio floor announcements and Braille buttons.

name: Connolly Station
description: Dublin's main intercity rail terminus and a key DART and commuter hub.
accessibilityNotes: Platform-to-concourse lifts available at all platforms; staff at assistance desk can arrange ramp boarding for intercity trains with 24hr notice; accessible toilet located near main ticket hall.

name: Heuston Station
description: Principal terminus for intercity services to Cork, Limerick, Galway and the west.
accessibilityNotes: Refurbished concourse has level flooring throughout; all intercity platforms accessible via lift; portable boarding ramps deployed by staff for train entry — advance notice strongly recommended.

name: Dublin Airport Terminal 1
description: The original terminal at Dublin Airport handling short-haul and charter flights.
accessibilityNotes: Dedicated assistance desk on arrivals level; ambulift vehicles serve aircraft without jet bridges; hidden disability sunflower lanyards available at information desks; accessible changing places toilet with hoist in arrivals.

name: Dublin Airport Terminal 2
description: Modern terminal at Dublin Airport serving transatlantic and major European routes.
accessibilityNotes: Opened 2010 to universal design standards; most gates served by level jet bridges; changing places toilet with adult hoist on departures level; electric buggy service available throughout piers.

name: The Convention Centre Dublin
description: Ireland's premier conference and events venue situated on the north Liffey quays.
address: Spencer Dock, North Wall Quay, Dublin 1
accessibilityNotes: All seven levels connected by panoramic glass lifts; reserved wheelchair spaces and companion seats available in all auditoriums; induction loop fitted in main plenary hall; accessible parking validated on request.

name: Aviva Stadium
description: Ireland's national rugby and football stadium, home to IRFU and FAI.
accessibilityNotes: 226 dedicated wheelchair spaces spread across all four stands with adjacent companion seats; accessible entry via turnstile-free gates at East Stand; stewards trained in disability awareness; accessible toilets at each concourse level.

name: Croke Park Stadium
description: Headquarters of the GAA and one of Europe's largest sports stadia.
accessibilityNotes: Over 300 wheelchair spaces across Cusack, Davin, Hogan and Hill 16 stands; lifts to all levels of new Canal End stand; accessible parking in on-site car park with blue badge bays adjacent to Davin Stand entrance.

name: National Museum of Ireland – Archaeology
description: Houses Ireland's most important archaeological collection including Viking and Bronze Age artefacts.
accessibilityNotes: Accessible entrance via side ramp on Kildare Street; Victorian building retrofitted with lift connecting all floors; ground floor galleries fully level; upper gallery lift is small and advance notice aids staff deployment.

name: National Museum of Ireland – Natural History
description: Victorian 'Dead Zoo' featuring Ireland's wildlife and global zoological specimens.
accessibilityNotes: Historic cast-iron upper gallery has no lift access; ground floor collections fully accessible via front ramp; upper floor balcony viewable only by those who can use stairs — a significant limitation for this Victorian building.

name: National Gallery of Ireland
description: National collection of fine and decorative arts spanning six centuries.
accessibilityNotes: Millennium Wing entrance on Clare Street is fully step-free; Dargan Wing accessible via lift from ground; free wheelchair loan available at information desk; audio descriptions available for selected works via gallery app.

name: Irish Museum of Modern Art (IMMA)
description: Ireland's leading modern and contemporary art museum set in the historic Royal Hospital Kilmainham.
address: Royal Hospital, Military Rd, Kilmainham, Dublin 8
accessibilityNotes: Ground floor of historic building is largely step-free; cobblestone courtyard has been supplemented with a smooth accessible path around its perimeter; lift to upper galleries is compact but functional for standard wheelchair widths.

name: Chester Beatty Library
description: World-class collection of manuscripts, prints, and rare books from Asia, the Middle East and Europe.
accessibilityNotes: Dedicated accessible entrance on Ship Street Great bypasses castle cobblestones; all three gallery floors served by lift; roof garden accessible via lift and has level paving with resting benches.

name: Guinness Storehouse
description: Iconic seven-storey visitor experience tracing the history of Ireland's most famous stout.
accessibilityNotes: All seven floors accessible via large panoramic lift; Gravity Bar at summit offers reserved wheelchair positions with 360° city views; entrance on Market Street is step-free with automatic doors; some exhibits have viewing height optimised for seated visitors.

name: Trinity College Dublin – Main Campus
description: Ireland's oldest university founded in 1592, home to the Book of Kells.
accessibilityNotes: Main cobblestone Front Square is challenging; smooth accessible routes mapped via TCD's disability service — pick up map at front gate; Book of Kells accessible via lift in Old Library; accessible toilets in Arts Block and Hamilton Building.

name: University College Dublin – Belfield
description: Main campus of Ireland's largest university, spanning 133 acres in south Dublin.
accessibilityNotes: Campus Accessibility Map available from UCD Access & Lifelong Learning office; all key lecture buildings connected by paved accessible routes; older 1960s blocks retrofitted with external ramps of varying gradient.

name: Dublin City University – Glasnevin
description: Modern technological university on the northside of Dublin with strong industry links.
accessibilityNotes: Purpose-built open-plan campus with largely level pathways; U-shaped drop-off zone adjacent to Henry Grattan Building; newer buildings have automatic doors and wide lift cars; some campus paths on gradient near sports complex.

name: St. Stephen's Green
description: Historic 22-acre public park and garden at the top of Grafton Street in Dublin city centre.
accessibilityNotes: Main entrance on the north side is step-free; central paved pathway loop is fully accessible; grass areas are uneven; accessible toilet near main gate requires attendant key available at park office; duck pond area has flush kerbs.

name: Phoenix Park
description: One of the largest enclosed city parks in Europe, covering 1,752 acres.
accessibilityNotes: Main road through park is tarmac and accessible; visitor centre and café are step-free; grass deer fields are uneven and unsuitable for manual wheelchairs in wet conditions; accessible toilet block near Áras an Uachtaráin gate.

name: Merrion Square Park
description: Elegant Georgian park in Dublin's embassy quarter with Oscar Wilde statue.
accessibilityNotes: All four entrances have flush-kerb access; interior gravel paths are compacted and accessible in dry conditions; no accessible toilet on-site — nearest is in National Gallery; paths widen at sculpture trail stops.

name: Herbert Park
description: Quiet south Dublin park with tennis courts, a pond and well-maintained green spaces.
accessibilityNotes: Tarmac pathway circles the park perimeter and is fully wheelchair accessible; accessible toilet near the pond is open during park hours; tennis spectator area has level viewing space; playground has accessible surfacing.

name: Marlay Park
description: Large Rathfarnham park popular for walking, the Wicklow Way trailhead, and summer concerts.
accessibilityNotes: Tarmac inner loop suitable for wheelchairs and powerchairs; accessible playground with Mobi-Mat surface near main car park; accessible toilet block near craft courtyard; outdoor concert venue has level ground section with designated wheelchair area.

name: Grafton Street
description: Dublin's premier pedestrianised shopping street running from College Green to St. Stephen's Green.
accessibilityNotes: Fully pedestrianised and level; most major retailers have automatic doors and step-free entrances; street furniture creates some narrow pinch points; dropped kerbs at cross streets; some side entrances to shops on Duke Street still have steps.

name: St. Stephen's Green Shopping Centre
description: Victorian-style glass-roofed shopping centre adjacent to St. Stephen's Green.
accessibilityNotes: Multi-storey glass atrium served by glass lifts on both sides; accessible toilets on first and second floor; car park on basement level with wide blue badge bays and lift to all floors; decorative floor tiles are smooth and level.

name: Dundrum Town Centre
description: Largest shopping centre in Ireland with over 100 stores across two levels.
accessibilityNotes: Eleven passenger lifts connecting all levels; changing rooms in anchor stores are wide-access; blue badge parking on all car park levels with level access to mall; accessible toilets with changing benches on both mall levels.

name: Liffey Valley Shopping Centre
description: Major retail park and enclosed mall in west Dublin, close to the M50 motorway.
accessibilityNotes: Single-level external retail park is fully step-free; enclosed mall has lifts to upper dining level; designated blue badge parking within 50m of all mall entrances; accessible toilet facilities on both mall levels.

name: Blanchardstown Shopping Centre
description: Expansive shopping and leisure complex in north-west Dublin.
accessibilityNotes: Vast single-level ground floor with accessible routes throughout; cinema complex on upper level has lift access; accessible toilet with adult changing table near food court; free wheelchair loan service at customer service desk.

name: Arnotts Department Store
description: Dublin's oldest department store, spanning a full city block on Henry Street.
accessibilityNotes: Multiple street-level entrances all step-free; large passenger lifts on Henry Street and Abbey Street sides; accessible toilet on third floor near restaurant; cosmetics counters feature lowered service points.

name: Brown Thomas
description: Upscale Irish department store occupying a prime Grafton Street address.
accessibilityNotes: Main Grafton Street entrance is step-free with automatic doors; lifts connect four trading floors; accessible toilet on second floor; some beauty counters in older section of store have raised plinths that limit wheelchair approach.

name: The Westbury Hotel
description: Five-star hotel in Dublin 2, steps from Grafton Street and Brown Thomas.
accessibilityNotes: Accessible rooms with roll-in shower, fold-down shower seat and emergency pull cord; lift services all guest floors; concierge drop-off directly at step-free entrance on Balfe Street; accessible toilet in ground floor lobby.

name: The Shelbourne Hotel
description: Historic five-star hotel overlooking St. Stephen's Green, a Dublin institution since 1824.
accessibilityNotes: Historic entrance has a short ramp alongside steps; accessible rooms on lower floors available on request; lift is narrow in the original wing but wider in the modern extension — specify accessibility needs at booking; Lord Mayor's Lounge is step-free.

name: Clayton Hotel Burlington Road
description: Large four-star conference hotel near Leeson Street and the Grand Canal.
accessibilityNotes: Four fully adapted guest rooms with wet-room shower, lowered wardrobe rails and accessible vanity; ground floor is fully step-free; blue badge parking spaces at entrance; all meeting rooms on ground floor accessible.

name: Maldron Hotel Cardiff Lane
description: Contemporary four-star hotel beside the Grand Canal Dock in the Silicon Docks area.
address: Cardiff Lane, Grand Canal Dock, Dublin 2
accessibilityNotes: Fully step-free lobby and bar area; two accessible rooms with roll-in shower and grab rails; LUAS Red Line stop within 150m has level platform access; no on-site parking but drop-off space directly at entrance.

name: Ibis Dublin City
description: Budget hotel conveniently located near the IFSC and Connolly Station.
accessibilityNotes: Single step at entrance mitigated by portable ramp; two accessible rooms with grab rails but no roll-in shower — wet room option available on request; lift to all floors is standard width.

name: Tesco Extra Phibsborough
description: Large-format Tesco supermarket in the Phibsborough Shopping Centre, north Dublin.
accessibilityNotes: Fully step-free entrance from shopping centre; motorised shopping scooters available at customer service desk; checkout lanes include two wide accessible tills; accessible toilet adjacent to customer services; blue badge parking adjacent to store entrance.

name: Lidl Tallaght
description: Discount supermarket serving the Tallaght and Citywest area of south-west Dublin.
accessibilityNotes: Standard Lidl layout with wide aisles and flat flooring throughout; no in-store accessible toilet; blue badge parking bays directly outside main entrance; checkout includes one wide-clearance till but no lowered service point.

name: Dunnes Stores Cornelscourt
description: Flagship Dunnes Stores supermarket and clothing outlet on the south Dublin N11 corridor.
accessibilityNotes: Accessible entrance via ramp at north side of building; trolleys available with child/bag holder accessible for wheelchair users; accessible toilet inside store near fitting rooms; customer service desk at lowered height.

name: Waitrose-style M&S Foodhall Dundrum
description: Marks & Spencer food hall and clothing store at Dundrum Town Centre.
accessibilityNotes: Accessed via Dundrum Town Centre lifts; store floor is fully level; wider checkout lanes designated for accessibility; part of overall shopping centre accessibility infrastructure.

name: Centra Rathmines
description: Neighbourhood convenience store on the busy Rathmines Road.
accessibilityNotes: Narrow shopfloor layout with some aisle restrictions; no accessible toilet; entrance is level from pavement; staff willing to assist but the store's compact footprint makes navigation difficult in a powered wheelchair.

name: Boots Pharmacy Grafton Street
description: Large Boots health and beauty pharmacy on the pedestrianised Grafton Street.
accessibilityNotes: Fully step-free from Grafton Street; wide aisles on ground and first floor; prescription counter has a lowered section; no accessible toilet on-site; lift to first floor pharmacy consultation room.

name: Hickey's Pharmacy Drumcondra
description: Local community pharmacy serving the Drumcondra and Glasnevin neighbourhoods.
accessibilityNotes: Portable ramp available at entrance — staff will deploy on request; single step at door is the main barrier; narrow interior requires care with larger wheelchairs; prescription dispensary counter has lowered zone; no accessible toilet.

name: LloydsPharmacy Blanchardstown
description: Large pharmacy within the Blanchardstown Shopping Centre.
accessibilityNotes: Fully accessible as part of shopping centre; wide aisles and a full-width lowered prescription counter; private consultation room accessible via step-free corridor; accessible toilet available in nearby shopping centre facilities.

name: GPO Dublin
description: Ireland's General Post Office on O'Connell Street, a historic landmark and active post office.
accessibilityNotes: Side ramp on Henry Street bypasses the portico steps; interior concourse is level; all service counters include lowered positions for wheelchair users; hearing loop at all counters; GPO Museum upstairs not accessible by lift.

name: An Post Rathmines Delivery Office
description: Local post office and parcel collection point serving the Rathmines area.
accessibilityNotes: Level entrance from car park; service counter has a fully lowered section; no accessible toilet; parcel collection hatch is at a reachable height for wheelchair users; some queuing space is limited during peak times.

name: Bank of Ireland College Green
description: Former Irish Parliament building, now a Bank of Ireland flagship branch.
accessibilityNotes: Historic building with side ramp access bypassing main portico steps; ATM at accessible height on College Green facade; interior counter has hearing loop; some areas of listed interior have uneven heritage flooring.

name: AIB Baggot Street
description: AIB branch in the heart of Dublin's financial district near Baggot Street Bridge.
accessibilityNotes: Modern refurbished branch with fully step-free entrance and wide accessible ATM vestibule; self-service machines at accessible height; private meeting room for accessible appointments available on ground floor.

name: Dublin City Council Civic Offices
description: The Wood Quay headquarters of Dublin City Council, housing planning and housing departments.
accessibilityNotes: Public entrance on Wood Quay is fully step-free; reception desk has an induction loop; lift access to all floors; accessible toilet on ground floor near reception; CCTV intercom at entrance allows staff to assist with access.

name: Department of Social Protection – Intreo Centre Gardiner St
description: Dublin City Intreo Centre providing employment and social welfare services.
address: Kings Inn House, 200 Parnell St, Dublin 1
accessibilityNotes: All floors accessible by lift; front entrance has step-free access with automatic door; service windows have an accessible low-level position; accessible toilet on ground floor; numbered ticketing system visible from wheelchair height.

name: Four Courts
description: Ireland's principal courts complex on the north quays of the River Liffey.
accessibilityNotes: Accessible entrance via side gate on Morgan Place with buzzer entry; dome rotunda has smooth marble flooring but can be slippery when wet; lifts to upper court rooms; accessible toilet on ground floor near security desk.

name: Leinster House
description: Seat of the Oireachtas (Irish Parliament), comprising Dáil Éireann and Seanad Éireann.
accessibilityNotes: Public gallery access via lift from Merrion Square entrance; Visitors Centre on Kildare Street has step-free access; Dáil and Seanad public galleries have wheelchair viewing positions; accessible toilet in Visitors Centre.

name: Dublin Castle
description: Former British administrative centre, now a key state ceremonial and tourism venue.
accessibilityNotes: Accessible tour route avoids main cobblestone courtyard; State Apartments accessible via lift from Lower Yard; Record Tower exterior only for those with mobility impairments; accessible toilet in Revenue building near Ship Street entrance.

name: Kilmainham Gaol
description: Historic 19th-century prison, site of the 1916 Rising executions and now a major museum.
accessibilityNotes: Accessible tour route covers main hall, East Wing and execution yard; upper East Wing cells not accessible — lift available for main viewing level; accessible toilet at reception; cobblestone sections of yard challenging for manual wheelchairs.

name: EPIC The Irish Emigration Museum
description: Award-winning underground museum exploring the Irish diaspora across 20 immersive galleries.
address: CHQ Building, Custom House Quay, Dublin 1
accessibilityNotes: Entirely underground, accessed by lift from CHQ Building ground floor; all 20 galleries on a single level with smooth flooring; interactive exhibits designed at accessible heights; audio guide includes hearing loop compatibility.

name: The Little Museum of Dublin
description: Charming Georgian townhouse museum documenting Dublin life in the 20th century.
accessibilityNotes: Victorian townhouse format means upper floors are inaccessible by wheelchair; ground floor exhibitions are accessible and staff can provide a tailored tour of ground-level content; step at entrance mitigated by portable ramp on request.

name: Dublin City Library – Pearse Street
description: Largest Dublin City Council public library, serving the city centre.
accessibilityNotes: Full step-free access from Pearse Street via ramp; lift to all three floors; reading room seating adjustable with tables at accessible height; accessible toilet on first floor; staff trained to assist with finding accessible formats.

name: Cabra Public Library
description: Community library serving the Cabra neighbourhood in north Dublin.
accessibilityNotes: Compact single-storey building with step-free entrance and ramp from car park; all collections on one level; accessible toilet accessible from reading room; no lift required as fully single-storey layout.

name: Ballyfermot Public Library
description: Community library in the Ballyfermot area of west Dublin.
accessibilityNotes: Single-level accessible library with ramp from street; standard accessible toilet near reception desk; compact layout with clear through-routes; no elevator needed due to single-storey design.

name: St. Patrick's Cathedral
description: Ireland's largest cathedral, founded in 1191 and closely associated with Jonathan Swift.
accessibilityNotes: Accessible entrance via west door ramp; nave flooring is historic stone — level but uneven in places; dedicated wheelchair seating at the front of nave and in transepts; induction loop throughout; accessible toilet near visitor entrance gift shop.

name: Christ Church Cathedral
description: Medieval cathedral on a hilltop in Dublin 8, founded circa 1030.
accessibilityNotes: Main entrance is at street level but interior crypt has step access; lift available to nave from accessible side entrance; medieval floors are uneven; wheelchair seating reserved at rear of nave; crypt exhibition partially accessible.

name: Whitefriar Street Church
description: Carmelite church in Dublin 2 famous for housing a relic of St. Valentine.
accessibilityNotes: Street-level entrance from Aungier Street is step-free; interior aisles are wide enough for manual wheelchairs; hearing loop in main body of church; side chapels have no steps; sacristy accessible for those needing quiet space.

name: Glasnevin Cemetery
description: Ireland's national cemetery and a major heritage attraction containing over 1.5 million burials.
accessibilityNotes: Main heritage cemetery has tarmac accessible pathways through key sections; guided tours available in accessible format with accessible buggy on request; Museum building is fully step-free; many grave sections off accessible paths are on gravel.

name: Dublin Zoo
description: One of the world's oldest zoos, located within Phoenix Park.
accessibilityNotes: Smooth tarmac pathways throughout most of zoo; accessible viewing platforms at African Savanna and Gorilla Rainforest; accessible toilets near Discovery Centre and Family Centre; wheelchair hire available at entrance; some hillside paths to Orangutan Forest have steeper gradients.

name: Botanic Gardens Glasnevin
description: National Botanic Gardens of Ireland with glasshouses and 20,000 plant species.
accessibilityNotes: Main tarmac paths throughout gardens are wide and level; Great Palm House fully accessible via ramp; gravel paths in older sections of garden are compact but firm; accessible toilet near main entrance; free wheelchair loan at reception.

name: Howth Cliff Walk
description: Scenic cliff-top walking route on the Howth Peninsula overlooking Dublin Bay.
accessibilityNotes: Lower cliff walk along the harbour is tarmac and accessible; upper and west cliff paths are narrow gravel trails with steep drops unsuitable for wheelchairs; Howth Harbour promenade is fully accessible with sea views as an alternative.

name: Sandymount Strand
description: Long sandy beach south-east of Dublin city centre, known for views of Dublin Bay.
accessibilityNotes: Promenade path along strand is tarmac and fully accessible; beach itself is soft sand with no beach wheelchair hire available; accessible toilet at Sandymount Green car park; dropped kerbs at all promenade crossing points.

name: Bull Island Beach (North Bull Island)
description: Protected UNESCO Biosphere Reserve and Blue Flag beach on a tidal island in north Dublin Bay.
accessibilityNotes: Causeway road is accessible by car; boardwalk nature trail is fully accessible with smooth timber surface; main beach is soft sand — a beach wheelchair is available for loan from Raheny Library by advance booking; accessible toilet at car park.

name: Seapoint Beach
description: Popular swimming spot and bathing place between Monkstown and Dún Laoghaire.
accessibilityNotes: Ramp to beach area from car park; no pool hoist or beach wheelchair available; seafront promenade accessible from Dún Laoghaire along flat coastal path; accessible toilet nearby at Dún Laoghaire waterfront.

name: Portmarnock Beach
description: Blue Flag beach on the north Dublin coast with a long sandy stretch and dune system.
accessibilityNotes: Level tarmac car park with blue badge spaces; no formal accessible route to beach — soft sand and dune crossings are impassable in standard wheelchairs; accessible toilet in beach car park is open seasonally.

name: Dalkey Castle and Heritage Centre
description: Medieval town castle offering heritage tours and theatrical performances in south Dublin.
accessibilityNotes: Ground floor heritage exhibition is accessible via ramp; castle tower and upper floors accessible by stairs only; staff can provide a seated audio commentary for visitors unable to access upper levels; accessible toilet nearby on Castle Street.

name: Dún Laoghaire Pier
description: Famous Victorian granite pier extending 1.2km into Dublin Bay from Dún Laoghaire harbour.
accessibilityNotes: East Pier is flat tarmac and fully accessible for its full 1.2km length; West Pier has some uneven granite sections; approach road from seafront has dropped kerbs; accessible parking available at People's Park nearby.

name: DART – Lansdowne Road Station
description: DART station serving Ballsbridge and the Aviva Stadium.
accessibilityNotes: Low-level platform ramp for boarding; no lift on this station; gaps between train and platform are bridged by portable ramp deployed by staff on request; accessible toilet not available on-site.

name: LUAS Cross City – O'Connell – GPO Stop
description: Central LUAS Green Line tram stop on O'Connell Street at the GPO.
accessibilityNotes: LUAS trams have level boarding from raised platform; all ticket machines on O'Connell Street stop have accessible height controls and audio output; platform surface is firm and level; tram carriages have dedicated wheelchair bays.

name: LUAS Red Line – Fatima Stop
description: LUAS Red Line tram stop serving the Rialto and Fatima areas of south Dublin.
accessibilityNotes: Open-air platform at street level with level tram boarding; tactile edge warning strips on platform; ticket machine has accessibility mode button; shelter seating includes armrest-free section; no step into tram from raised platform.

name: Dublin Bus – O'Connell Street Stop 339
description: Major Dublin Bus interchange stop on O'Connell Street northbound.
accessibilityNotes: Level pavement access to bus stop shelter; Real Time Passenger Information display at accessible viewing height; all buses serving this stop are low-floor with kneeling capability and fold-down ramp; audio destination announcements on board.

name: Odeon Cinema Point Village
description: Multiplex cinema at Point Village, near the 3Arena in the north docklands.
address: Point Square, North Wall Quay, Dublin 1
accessibilityNotes: All seven screens accessible via lift; each screen has dedicated wheelchair spaces with companion seating at varying heights; hearing loop in all screens; accessible toilet on each level; on-screen accessibility symbols on booking site for seat selection.

name: The Lighthouse Cinema
description: Independent arthouse cinema in the heart of Smithfield, Dublin 7.
accessibilityNotes: Step-free entrance from Smithfield Square via ramp; lift to all four screens; wheelchair spaces at the front of each screen with companion seating adjacent; hearing loop in all screens; accessible toilet on ground floor.

name: The 3Arena
description: Ireland's largest indoor music and entertainment venue on the north docklands.
accessibilityNotes: Dedicated accessible entrance on east side with level approach; over 100 wheelchair viewing platforms spread across floor, lower tier and upper tier; companion seats adjacent to all wheelchair positions; changing places toilet available; vibrating wristbands for deaf/hard of hearing available at box office.

name: Vicar Street
description: Mid-size live music venue in the Liberties, known for intimate gigs and comedy shows.
accessibilityNotes: Accessible entrance via side ramp on Thomas Street; wheelchair area at stage left on ground floor with 10 spaces and companion seating; accessible toilet behind main bar; hearing loop in main hall; bar counter not lowered but staff assist wheelchair users.

name: The Abbey Theatre
description: Ireland's national theatre on Abbey Street, staging new and classic Irish drama.
accessibilityNotes: Step-free entrance from Abbey Street; wheelchair spaces in stalls with companion seating; Peacock Theatre downstairs accessible via lift; audio described and captioned performances scheduled every season; accessible toilet adjacent to foyer bar.

name: The Gate Theatre
description: Historic Georgian theatre on Cavendish Row, known for international drama and world premieres.
accessibilityNotes: Side ramp entrance on Parnell Square East; four wheelchair spaces in stalls row A with adjacent companion seat; historic 1929 building retrofitted with hearing loop; stairs to circle — no lift to upper seating; accessible toilet on ground floor.

name: Science Gallery Dublin
description: Free contemporary science and art exhibition space at Trinity College Dublin.
accessibilityNotes: Accessible entrance from Pearse Street separate to main TCD cobblestone route; lift to upper gallery level; all interactive exhibits at accessible heights; accessible toilet on ground floor; exhibition notes available in large print.

name: Wagamama Dundrum
description: Japanese-inspired noodle restaurant within Dundrum Town Centre.
accessibilityNotes: Accessed via fully accessible shopping centre routes; level restaurant floor with wide bench seating and space for wheelchairs at end of tables; accessible toilet via shopping centre facilities; no threshold at restaurant entrance.

name: Etto Restaurant
description: Neighbourhood restaurant on Merrion Row serving Italian-inspired Irish seasonal cooking.
accessibilityNotes: Single step at front entrance — no ramp available; narrow interior with closely spaced tables limits wheelchair manoeuvrability; staff are accommodating but the compact room layout means access is challenging; accessible toilet not on-site.

name: The Winding Stair Restaurant
description: Iconic Dublin restaurant and bookshop in a Georgian building overlooking the Ha'penny Bridge.
accessibilityNotes: Bookshop on ground floor is accessible but the restaurant is on the first floor with a narrow winding staircase — no lift; ground floor café seating near window offers partial river views for those unable to use stairs.

name: Brasserie Sixty6
description: Modern Dublin brasserie on Great George's Street serving brunch, lunch and dinner.
accessibilityNotes: Fully level entrance from George's Street; spacious dining room with wheelchair-accessible table positions near the front; accessible toilet on ground floor; no raised sections in the dining area.

name: Bewley's Café Grafton Street
description: Dublin's most famous café, operating since 1927 in a stunning Art Nouveau building.
accessibilityNotes: Ground floor café is level and accessible; upper balcony seating reached by ornate staircase or lift; accessible toilet on first floor; historic stained glass interior creates some narrow routes between tables.

name: Siopaella Designer Exchange
description: Pre-loved designer fashion boutique in a Georgian basement on Drury Street.
accessibilityNotes: Shop located in a basement below street level, accessible by stairs only; no ramp or lift is available; wheelchair users are unable to access the premises.

name: Gutter Bookshop
description: Beloved independent bookshop in the heart of Temple Bar.
accessibilityNotes: Level entrance from Cow's Lane cobblestone street; interior is compact with narrow shelf rows — passable in a narrow manual wheelchair but tight in a powered chair; no accessible toilet on-site; no steps inside the shop.

name: Chapter One Restaurant
description: Michelin-starred basement restaurant under the Dublin Writers Museum on Parnell Square.
accessibilityNotes: Basement location served by a lift from street level; front of house will operate lift on request; wide spacing between tables allows wheelchair access; accessible toilet adjacent to dining room; advance notice recommended to ensure lift is ready on arrival.

name: 3FE Coffee Grand Canal Street
description: Flagship branch of Dublin's celebrated specialty coffee roaster in the Grand Canal area.
accessibilityNotes: Fully step-free entrance from Grand Canal Street; open-plan industrial interior with ample turning space; bar-style seating along the window is not wheelchair suitable but ground-level tables accommodate wheelchair users; no accessible toilet on-site.

name: The Fumbally Café
description: Neighbourhood café and community space in the Liberties known for wholesome food and events.
accessibilityNotes: Side entrance on Fumbally Lane is step-free via a short ramp; large open warehouse-style interior with wide aisles; communal tables are at accessible height; no accessible toilet within the café.

name: Butlers Chocolate Café Grafton Street
description: Irish chocolatier café on Dublin's premier shopping street.
accessibilityNotes: Step-free from pedestrianised Grafton Street; compact interior with some table spacing constraints; counter height is standard — no lowered service point; no accessible toilet; limited space for powered wheelchairs.

name: Starbucks Jervis Shopping Centre
description: Starbucks outlet inside the Jervis Shopping Centre in Dublin city centre.
address: Jervis Shopping Centre, Mary St, Dublin 1
accessibilityNotes: Fully accessible as part of Jervis Shopping Centre; counter has a lowered order point; accessible toilet available in the shopping centre nearby; spacious seating area with wide aisles between tables.

name: Clontarf Promenade
description: Seafront promenade along Clontarf Road with views across Bull Island to Howth.
accessibilityNotes: Flat tarmac seafront path extends 3km with no steps; accessible toilet at Dollymount car park area; benches and picnic tables with wheelchair clearance at regular intervals; dropped kerbs at all road crossings; bus accessible from city stops along the route.

name: Grand Canal Linear Park
description: Towpath park running along the Grand Canal from Dublin city centre to the suburbs.
accessibilityNotes: Tarmac towpath accessible along most stretches from Ringsend to Rathmines; entry points at Baggot Street Bridge, Leeson Street Bridge and Portobello have ramps; path surface is firm; some sections narrowed by parked cyclists.

name: Meánscoil Iognáid Rís – Belvédère College
description: Jesuit secondary school in Dublin 1 on Great Denmark Street.
accessibilityNotes: Main Georgian building retrofitted with ramp at side entrance and lift to classroom floors; accessible toilet in the 1990s extension; some historic areas remain step-restricted.

name: National Aquatic Centre
description: Ireland's premier aquatics facility with Olympic-sized pool, leisure pools and water slides.
accessibilityNotes: Two pool hoists available in the main pool and leisure pool; changing rooms include fully accessible wet room with fold-down shower seat; poolside transfer bench beside hoist; accessible changing village with adult-size changing bench; blue badge parking adjacent to entrance.

name: Fingal Lawn Tennis Club Malahide
description: Active tennis club on the north Dublin coast offering coaching and social tennis.
accessibilityNotes: Tarmac pathways to all courts; accessible toilet in main clubhouse; wheelchair tennis coaching available through advance arrangement with the club; spectator area beside courts is level.

name: Tolka Valley Park
description: Linear park following the Tolka River through north Dublin from Finglas to Glasnevin.
accessibilityNotes: Tarmac riverside path mostly level with gentle gradients; car park at Cardiffsbridge Road entry has blue badge spaces; accessible toilet near Cardiffsbridge car park open during park hours; picnic areas off main path have level access.

name: Blessington Street Basin
description: Hidden urban park and wildlife sanctuary surrounding a former city reservoir in Dublin 7.
accessibilityNotes: Ramp from Blessington Street into the basin park; inner perimeter path is tarmac and accessible; gated entrance is narrow — manual wheelchairs pass comfortably; powered wheelchairs may be restricted by gate width; no accessible toilet on-site.

name: Pearse Museum St. Enda's Park
description: Museum in the house of 1916 leader Patrick Pearse, set within a walled heritage park.
address: St Enda's Park, Grange Rd, Rathfarnham, Dublin 16
accessibilityNotes: Main house entrance accessible via side ramp; ground floor exhibition fully accessible; upstairs rooms accessible only by stairs; park pathways are tarmac and accessible; accessible toilet in visitor centre; nature trail in park has some gravel sections.

name: Irishtown Nature Park
description: Compact coastal nature park at the edge of the Liffey estuary near Ringsend.
accessibilityNotes: Flat tarmac paths through the nature reserve; car park with level access; no formal accessible toilet within the park — nearest at Ringsend Community Centre; bird-watching hide at the north end is accessible via smooth path.

name: Dublin Writers Museum
description: Museum celebrating the rich literary tradition of Dublin on Parnell Square.
accessibilityNotes: Side ramp to accessible entrance; lift to upper gallery floor; accessible toilet in the basement level; Georgian building with some narrow doorways in original sections — most exhibits accessible.

name: Hugh Lane Gallery
description: Dublin's municipal gallery of modern art in a Charlemont House on Parnell Square.
address: Charlemont House, Parnell Square N, Dublin 1
accessibilityNotes: Accessible entrance on Parnell Square via ramp; lift to all gallery floors including Francis Bacon Studio; accessible toilet on ground floor; audio guide available; most galleries have wide doorways and level flooring.

name: Teeling Whiskey Distillery
description: Working craft whiskey distillery and visitor centre in the Liberties, Dublin 8.
accessibilityNotes: Accessible entrance via ramp on Newmarket square; distillery tour accessible by lift between production floors; tasting room is level; accessible toilet on ground floor; some distillery equipment viewing is at floor level which suits wheelchair users.

name: Malahide Castle and Gardens
description: Medieval castle set in 260 acres of parkland on the north Dublin coast.
accessibilityNotes: Castle ground floor fully accessible via ramp; upper floors of the historic castle not accessible by lift; gardens have tarmac accessible paths around main formal areas; nature walk sections are on compacted gravel; accessible toilet near visitor centre.

name: Newbridge House Donabate
description: 18th-century country house with a working traditional farm and walled garden.
accessibilityNotes: Ground floor of house accessible via external ramp; upper floors accessible by stairs only; accessible toilet in stable block visitor centre; farm yard paths are compacted gravel; picnic area level with accessible parking adjacent.

name: Dublin Public Toilet – College Green
description: Public toilet facility on College Green, opposite Trinity College.
accessibilityNotes: RADAR-key accessible cubicle within the public toilet block; key available from Dublin City Council offices; step-free access from College Green pavement; facility maintained by Dublin City Council.

name: Jervis Street Car Park Accessible Toilet
description: Accessible public toilet within the Jervis Shopping Centre car park.
address: Jervis Shopping Centre, Mary St, Dublin 1
accessibilityNotes: RADAR-key operated accessible toilet on car park level P1; accessible via lift from shopping centre; clean and well-maintained; open during shopping centre hours only.

name: St. Enda's Swimming Pool Rathfarnham
description: Public swimming pool operated by Rathfarnham Area Partnership.
accessibilityNotes: Pool hoist available for main pool; ambulant disabled changing room with fold-down bench and grab rails; accessible shower adjacent to changing area; hoist must be booked 24hrs in advance through reception; step-free entrance from car park.

name: Ballsbridge Medical Centre
description: Private GP and specialist medical centre in Dublin 4.
accessibilityNotes: Side ramp to entrance; lift to all consultation floors; accessible toilet on ground floor; hearing loop at reception desk; standard-width examination rooms on ground floor accommodate wheelchairs.

name: Rotunda Hospital
description: World's oldest continuously operating maternity hospital, located on Parnell Square.
accessibilityNotes: Accessible entrance on Parnell Square via automated door and ramp; lift to all wards and outpatient clinics; accessible toilet in main reception area; some older ward corridors are narrower; staff dedicated to patient assistance throughout.

name: Children's Health Ireland at Crumlin
description: Ireland's main paediatric hospital, providing specialist care for children across the country.
address: Our Lady's Children's Hospital, Crumlin, Dublin 12
accessibilityNotes: All major wards and outpatient areas accessible by lift; specialist equipment including ceiling hoists in adapted rooms; accessible family bathroom on each ward; blue badge parking in dedicated bays adjacent to main entrance; patient transport co-ordination service available.

name: Dundrum Library
description: Dublin City Council public library serving the Dundrum and Churchtown area.
accessibilityNotes: Single-storey modern library extension is fully accessible via ramp; older wing has slight threshold step; accessible toilet near main reading room; hearing loop at the service desk; book return slot at wheelchair-accessible height.

name: Fingal County Council Offices
description: Administrative headquarters of Fingal County Council serving north county Dublin.
address: County Hall, Main St, Swords, Co. Dublin
accessibilityNotes: Modern county hall fully step-free; all service counters at accessible height; induction loop at planning and housing service desks; car park with blue badge bays adjacent to building; accessible toilet on each floor.

name: Swords Town Centre
description: Outdoor pedestrian shopping area and Main Street in Swords, Co. Dublin.
accessibilityNotes: Newly pedestrianised Main Street has smooth tarmac surface with flush kerbs throughout; accessible parking nearby on Church Road; most major shops have automatic or easily opened doors; dropped kerbs at bus stops along route.

name: Airfield Estate Dundrum
description: Urban farm, café and gardens open to the public in the heart of Dundrum.
accessibilityNotes: Farm pathways are tarmac and mostly level with some gentle slopes; accessible toilet in main visitor building; accessible parking adjacent to entrance; orchard and kitchen garden sections have firm gravel paths; café area fully level and accessible.

name: Rathfarnham Castle
description: Restored 16th-century castle now managed by the OPW as a heritage site.
accessibilityNotes: Ramp at side entrance; OPW installed a platform lift for castle interior; accessible toilet in visitor reception area; some rooms have high door thresholds typical of castle construction.

name: Clondalkin Round Tower Park
description: Small heritage park in Clondalkin village, featuring one of Ireland's best-preserved round towers.
accessibilityNotes: Park entrance on Tower Road is step-free; path around the round tower is flat tarmac; no accessible toilet within the park; viewing area of tower exterior is accessible; tower interior not open to visitors.

name: Spar Convenience Store Ranelagh
description: Busy neighbourhood Spar on the main Ranelagh Road.
accessibilityNotes: Level entrance from footpath; very narrow aisle layout makes independent navigation almost impossible in a standard wheelchair; no accessible toilet; staff generally available to assist but limited floor space is a structural constraint.

name: Dún Laoghaire Baths
description: Restored Victorian sea baths and lido at Dún Laoghaire, now a public bathing facility.
address: Marine Rd, Dún Laoghaire, Co. Dublin
accessibilityNotes: Newly restored facility with fully accessible changing rooms and wet room shower; pool hoist available for outdoor tidal pool; step-free entrance from Marine Road; accessible toilet in main changing block; staff trained in water-based accessibility support.

name: Screen Cinema Dublin (IFI)
description: Irish Film Institute arthouse cinema in Temple Bar.
accessibilityNotes: Step-free entrance via ramp on Eustace Street; lift to both cinema screens; dedicated wheelchair spaces in rows with companion seating; hearing loops in both screens; accessible toilet on ground floor near bar; IFI access card for regular visitors available.

name: GAA Museum at Croke Park
description: Interactive museum charting the history of Gaelic games, located within Croke Park stadium.
accessibilityNotes: Accessed from Croke Park stadium entrance; lift to all museum levels; stadium tour route for non-match days includes accessible routes to pitch-side and press box via lifts; accessible toilet adjacent to museum entrance; audio commentary available.

name: Bord Gáis Energy Theatre
description: Purpose-built modern theatre and concert venue in the Grand Canal Dock area.
accessibilityNotes: State-of-the-art accessible venue; 26 wheelchair spaces on stalls level with adjacent companion seats; further wheelchair positions in the dress circle reachable by lift; captioned and audio-described performances scheduled each season; accessible parking in nearby Grand Canal Square car park.

name: Tallaght Stadium
description: Home ground of Shamrock Rovers FC in Tallaght, south-west Dublin.
accessibilityNotes: Wheelchair viewing section in south stand with 40 spaces and companion seating at match height; accessible toilet block adjacent to disabled section; blue badge parking on stadium access road; step-free approach from car park to viewing area.

name: National Sports Campus Abbotstown
description: Ireland's national sporting infrastructure hub housing multiple elite sporting bodies.
accessibilityNotes: Modern campus with accessible parking in all zones; indoor arena has lift access to viewing galleries; changing rooms are fully accessible with hoist points; pathway network between facilities is smooth tarmac.

name: Markievicz Leisure Centre
description: Dublin City Council public leisure centre with pool and gym near Townsend Street.
accessibilityNotes: Pool hoist installed at shallow end of main pool; accessible changing room with fold-down shower bench and emergency call cord; lift to gym level; step-free entrance from Townsend Street via ramp; accessible toilet near reception.

name: Morton Stadium Santry
description: Athletics stadium in Santry, home of Irish athletics and site of the first sub-4-minute mile outside the UK.
accessibilityNotes: Accessible viewing area on main straight at trackside level; accessible toilet near main stand; blue badge parking at stadium entrance; no elevator to upper grandstand seating.

name: Ringsend Park
description: Local park in Ringsend with football pitches, playground and green space.
accessibilityNotes: Tarmac path around park perimeter is accessible; playground has rubberised surfacing and accessible equipment including a wheelchair-friendly roundabout; accessible toilet in the park pavilion open weekday hours; car park with blue badge spaces at Pigeon House Road entrance.

name: St. Anne's Park Raheny
description: Expansive north Dublin park famous for its rose gardens and woodland walks.
accessibilityNotes: Main tarmac roads through park accessible for wheelchairs and powerchairs; rose garden area is paved and step-free; accessible playground with Mobi-Mat surfacing near main car park; accessible toilet at rose garden pavilion; woodland trails are on natural surface.

name: Tallaght Cross West Shopping Centre
description: Large Tallaght shopping centre and entertainment complex in south-west Dublin.
accessibilityNotes: Large level shopping mall with wide corridors; multiple lifts throughout; accessible toilets with adult changing bench near food court; blue badge parking on all car park levels with level connection to mall; cinema complex has accessible seating.

name: Raheny Library
description: Public library serving the Raheny area of north Dublin, close to Bull Island.
accessibilityNotes: Single-storey library accessed via ramp from car park; fully level interior layout; accessible toilet near children's section; beach wheelchair for Bull Island available for loan at front desk with advance booking.

name: Marino Mart Pharmacy
description: Small independent pharmacy on Marino Mart serving the Marino neighbourhood.
accessibilityNotes: Level entrance from footpath; compact interior is navigable in a narrow manual wheelchair; dispensary counter has a lowered section; no accessible toilet; narrow aisles limit circulation for larger wheelchairs.

name: Merrion Shopping Centre
description: Neighbourhood shopping centre on the Merrion Road in Ballsbridge.
accessibilityNotes: Single-level strip mall with flat car park and step-free access to all units; accessible parking spaces closest to pharmacy and supermarket entrances; no lift required; no accessible toilet within the centre.

name: Dún Laoghaire Shopping Centre
description: Enclosed shopping centre in central Dún Laoghaire close to the ferry port.
address: Marine Rd, Dún Laoghaire, Co. Dublin
accessibilityNotes: Fully accessible enclosed mall with lift to upper and lower levels; accessible toilet on ground floor near customer services; free blue badge parking in on-site car park; accessible exit to Marine Road.

name: Mount Argus Church Harold's Cross
description: Passionist church and shrine to Blessed Charles of Mount Argus in Harold's Cross.
address: Lower Kimmage Rd, Harold's Cross, Dublin 6W
accessibilityNotes: Side entrance from the avenue is step-free via a short ramp; wide nave allows wheelchair access to front of church; dedicated seating spaces for wheelchair users in the side aisles; induction loop throughout the main church.

name: Pro Cathedral Dublin
description: The Catholic Metropolitan Cathedral of Dublin on Marlborough Street.
accessibilityNotes: Ramp at the south entrance on Cathedral Street; wide nave with clear wheelchair routes to communion areas; hearing loop throughout; accessible toilet in the lower sacristy; Sunday Mass has signed interpretation on the first Sunday of the month.

name: Harold's Cross Greyhound Stadium
description: Intimate greyhound racing venue in Dublin 6W offering evening race nights.
accessibilityNotes: Accessible entrance on Harold's Cross Road via ramp; reserved wheelchair viewing area trackside with companion seating; accessible toilet near main bar; car park with blue badge spaces close to entrance; restaurant on first floor has no lift access.

name: Shelbourne Park Greyhound Stadium
description: Ireland's most celebrated greyhound racing venue on the south docks.
accessibilityNotes: Step-free entrance on South Lotts Road; lift to restaurant floor and trackside hospitality suite; 12 wheelchair spaces trackside with companion seats; accessible toilet at ground level near betting area; blue badge parking in stadium forecourt.

name: Milltown Golf Club
description: Prestigious parkland golf club in the Milltown area of south Dublin.
accessibilityNotes: Clubhouse entrance accessible via ramp; accessible toilet in main changing area; fairways are uneven natural terrain unsuitable for standard wheelchairs — motorised golf buggy available for hire to traverse course.

name: Collins Barracks – Decorative Arts Museum
description: Branch of the National Museum of Ireland housing decorative arts and history collections.
accessibilityNotes: Accessible entrance via Benburb Street gate; lift in main exhibition building connects ground and first floor; cobblestone parade ground has a tarmac accessible path running parallel; accessible toilet near main reception; outdoor courtyard café is step-free.

name: Peamount Healthcare
description: Specialist rehabilitation hospital and community healthcare provider in west County Dublin.
accessibilityNotes: Purpose-built rehabilitation campus; all wards and therapy rooms accessible by lift; accessible parking directly outside main reception; campus paths connect all buildings without steps; specialist equipment throughout for patients with mobility impairments.

name: Mater Hospital
description: Major Dublin hospital and centre for cardiology, oncology and transplant services.
accessibilityNotes: Main entrance on Eccles Street is step-free with sliding automatic doors; all ward blocks connected by lift; accessible toilet in main reception area; blue badge parking on Eccles Street and in hospital car park; patient transport service available for those with mobility needs.

name: An Post sorting Office Portobello
description: An Post branch and parcel collection point serving the Portobello area.
accessibilityNotes: Portable ramp available on request from staff; single step at entrance is main access barrier; counter has one lowered service point for wheelchair users; queuing area is limited but manageable; no accessible toilet.

name: Spar Convenience Store IFSC
description: Convenient corner store in the International Financial Services Centre.
accessibilityNotes: Level entrance from IFSC plaza; modern layout with wider than average aisles for a convenience store; no accessible toilet; checkout is standard height with no lowered option.

name: Pearse DART Station
description: Central Dublin DART station near Trinity College and Grand Canal Dock.
accessibilityNotes: Lift access to both platforms from Westland Row entrance; portable boarding ramp deployed by staff for DART entry; accessible ticket machine at main entrance; accessible toilet not available on-site.

name: Grand Canal Dock DART Station
description: DART station serving the Grand Canal Dock and tech quarter of Dublin 2.
accessibilityNotes: Modern station with lift to both platforms; level boarding ramp available from station staff; real-time displays at accessible viewing height; close to LUAS stop for onward accessible travel.

name: Tara Street DART Station
description: Central Dublin DART station on the south quays near O'Connell Bridge.
accessibilityNotes: No lift at this station; ramp access to lower platform level but gap between platform and train requires staff-deployed portable ramp; accessible toilet not available; staff assistance must be arranged in advance for this station.

name: Richmond Barracks
description: Historic barracks in Inchicore, processing centre for 1916 Rising internees, now a community museum.
accessibilityNotes: Ground floor museum exhibition fully accessible; outdoor parade ground has smooth tarmac surface; accessible toilet in reception block; some interpretation panels at accessible viewing height.

name: Coolock Library
description: North Dublin community library serving the Coolock, Artane and Beaumont areas.
accessibilityNotes: Single-storey library with ramp from car park; all collections on one accessible level; accessible toilet behind service desk; talking book and large-print collection prominently located near entrance.

name: Walkinstown Library
description: Public library in the Walkinstown area of south-west Dublin.
accessibilityNotes: Single-storey building with ramp from street; level floor throughout; accessible toilet accessible from main reading area; no lift required; talking book collection available at accessible height.

name: Bus Éireann Busáras
description: Dublin's central bus station operated by Bus Éireann, serving national and cross-border routes.
accessibilityNotes: Main concourse is fully step-free with automatic entrance doors; departure gates accessible via level concourse; accessible toilet on ground floor near waiting area; staff assistance desk for passengers with disabilities; all National Express coaches have wheelchair accommodation with advance booking.

name: Farmleigh House and Estate
description: State guest house in Phoenix Park, open to the public on selected days.
accessibilityNotes: Accessible entrance to main house via side ramp; estate grounds have tarmac accessible paths around walled garden and lake; accessible toilet in courtyard near café; lift to main state rooms on first floor; guided tours follow accessible route.

name: Cliffs of Moher Visitor Experience Dublin Gateway
description: Tourism Ireland visitor information point in Dublin city centre for planning Wild Atlantic Way trips.
accessibilityNotes: Step-free from Suffolk Street pedestrian zone; single-level interior with wide aisle between display stands; service counter has lowered section for wheelchair users; hearing loop at desk; accessible toilet not on-site.

name: Kilbarrack Fire Station
description: Dublin Fire Brigade station serving the north coastal suburbs.
accessibilityNotes: Public reception area accessible via ramp; accessible toilet for staff and visitors; this station runs community fire safety visits and can accommodate wheelchair users; vehicle bay area not publicly accessible.

name: Holles Street National Maternity Hospital
description: Ireland's busiest maternity hospital in the heart of Dublin 2.
accessibilityNotes: Historic 1930s building with ramp to main entrance; lift access to all wards; accessible toilet near antenatal clinic; some older ward corridors narrow — staff assist as needed; outpatient area on ground floor is most accessible.

name: Dublin City University Sports Complex
description: Multi-sport facilities at Dublin City University open to students and public members.
accessibilityNotes: Full accessible changing rooms with wet room and grab rails; pool hoist at the leisure pool available with advance notice; step-free pathways between all sports facilities; lift to squash courts and gym upper level.

name: Clondalkin Library
description: Public library in Clondalkin village, Dublin 22.
accessibilityNotes: Ramp from car park to entrance; all library collections on single accessible level; accessible toilet near children's section; no lift required; braille and large-print materials available.

name: Ballymun Library and Civic Centre
description: Modern community library within the Ballymun Civic Centre.
accessibilityNotes: Civic Centre and library are fully step-free with automatic doors; lift to all levels; accessible toilet in library and in civic offices; hearing loop at service desk; modern building to universal design standard.
